summary: Replace the compact repository overview with an evidence-based GitHub landing page that makes the Windows beta easy to understand, verify, install, and evaluate without overstating M5 or M6 maturity.

decisions.decision: Lead with the signed Windows beta, source-first value, verified installation path, and existing project artwork; group implemented behavior by user workflow; expose architecture and release evidence; and identify every M5 and M6 cluster as planned in a compact status matrix.
decisions.rationale: A new visitor should understand and try the real release within one scan while retaining the exact boundary between the available M0-M4 beta and the complete M0-M6 product commitment. Repository artwork and implementation evidence communicate this without a fabricated screenshot or competitor claim.

testEvidence.criterion: The README must render as GitHub-compatible Markdown, expose a five-step first model path, preserve the exact release contract, and contain no broken local or public links.
testEvidence.test: Run the existing public-surface contract, mechanically validate every unique README destination, fetch every public destination once, and render the Markdown through GitHub's GFM API.

red.command: Repository-first landing-page inventory before editing README.md
red.outcome: RED: the accurate 59-line overview had no logo or hero artwork, no direct workflow narrative, no first-model example, no support matrix, no public release-evidence section, and only one compact architecture diagram; installation and maturity required a closer read than the requested repository landing-page standard permits.

green.command: pnpm.cmd check:public-surfaces; README relative-path/HTTP checker; gh api markdown -F text=@README.md -f mode=gfm -f context=scottconverse/scadmill
green.outcome: GREEN: the baseline public-surface contract passed 2/2 tests; the rewritten README had 27 unique destinations, 16 relative destinations, zero broken relative paths, and 11 public URLs returning HTTP 200; GitHub rendered one H1, six images/badges, and both Mermaid diagrams.

questions: No owner decision is required; the owner explicitly directed the implementer to apply the GitHub repository landing-page skill to this repository.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
